'''Challep''' was the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]]<ref name="Starfighter Trap" /> in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]].<ref name="TST Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Starfighter Trap]]'' to [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]].</ref> During this year, he sought to bolster Agamar's planetary defences, in particular the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] contingencies. He purchased technology of various groups including at least 100 [[Droid starfighter/Legends|Droid starfighters]] from [[Xi Char/Legends|Xi Char]] but also wanted the technology of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]{{'s}} [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighters]] and [[N-X Police Cruiser/Legends|N-X Police Cruisers]]. <ref name="Starfighter Trap" />

To this end, he found an opportunity by secretly contacting [[Dren Melne]] who spent years being a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] before returning to Naboo. Melne ensured him that even if [[Nubian/Legends|Nubians]] sold him the materials, they weren't enough to reach the Naboo's technology, unless it was replicated from working prototypes.<ref name="Starfighter Trap" />

After, at least, three attempts to purchase the fighters that were all denied by [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala{{'s}}]] [[Naboo Royal Advisory Council/Legends|Advisory Council]], he sought to steal them instead, and employed Melne for the job. Melne was disillusioned by his life as a pilot of the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps]], and saw it as an opportunity to return to Agamar.<ref name="Starfighter Trap" />

Soon a [[Hornet-class carrier]] [[Battle at TFP-9|attacked]] [[Station TFP-9]] as part of a "demonstration" to see if the fighters are as good as he heard. When the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Royal Space Fighter Corps]] sent 12 Police Cruisers and 2 N-1 Starfighters in response and managed to defeat the first [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] the second Hornet-class carrier, which had [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sorran]] of the ''[[Velumina]]'', arrived with another squad of Z-95s armed with [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] and Droid starfighters from the first carrier. Combined with Dren Melne switching sides to engage [[Echo Flight]], it was meant to make the odds so overwhelming to consider the fight foolish to continue.<ref name="Starfighter Trap" />

Despite the overwhelming odds, [[Essara Till/Legends|Essara Till]] in her own N-1, with [[Rhys Dallows]] who was piloting Police Cruiser [[Echo Five]] managed to destroy the first carrier, combined with other losses caused Captain Sorran to kill Dren to cover their tracks. Challep would later deny any involvement with the attack, although investigations regarding his recent investigations into purchases of fighter technology by [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Sio Bibble/Legends|Sio Bibble]] implied otherwise.<ref name="Starfighter Trap" />
